title: Fatigue Testing of Metallic Materials
description: Master schema representing the fatigue testing process for metallic materials, capturing inputs, parameters, equipment, and outputs.

material.description: Details of the metallic material being tested.

alloyDesignation.description: Standard designation of the alloy (e.g., AISI 4140, Al 2024-T3, Ti-6Al-4V).

chemicalComposition.description: Chemical composition of the material in weight percentage. Keys are element symbols, values are percentages. Optional, as standard alloys are sufficiently identified by alloyDesignation.

heatTreatmentCondition.description: Heat treatment condition of the material (e.g., Q&T, T3, mill-annealed, solution-annealed).

specimenGeometry.description: Physical dimensions and shape of the test specimen, conforming to standards such as ASTM E466 or ISO 1099.

notchCondition.description: Whether the specimen is notched or unnotched.

gaugeLength.description: Gauge length of the specimen in millimeters (mm).

crossSectionalArea.description: Cross-sectional area of the specimen in square millimeters (mm²).

loadingType.description: Mode of mechanical loading applied during the test.

loadingParameters.description: Parameters defining the loading conditions applied during testing. One of stressAmplitude or strainAmplitude applies depending on the test control type.

stressAmplitude.description: Stress amplitude (σ_a) in megapascals (MPa) for stress-controlled (HCF) tests.

strainAmplitude.description: Strain amplitude (ε_a) in mm/mm (dimensionless) for strain-controlled (LCF) tests.

stressRatio.description: Ratio of minimum to maximum applied stress (R = σ_min / σ_max), defining loading asymmetry. Common values: R = -1 (fully reversed), R = 0 (zero-to-tension), R = 0.1.

loadingFrequency.description: Number of load cycles applied per second (Hz). Ranges from <1 Hz for LCF to 20-60 Hz for conventional HCF, up to 20 kHz for ultrasonic VHCF testing.

testEnvironment.description: Environmental conditions during the test that may influence crack initiation and propagation.

atmosphere.description: Type of atmosphere during the test.

medium.description: Surrounding medium (e.g., ambient, NaCl solution). Especially relevant for corrosion fatigue.

equipment.description: Details of the testing equipment and standards used.

testingStandard.description: Normative procedure followed during testing (e.g., ASTM E466, ASTM E606, ISO 1099, DIN 50100, JIS Z 2273).

outputs.description: Results and outputs from the fatigue test.

numberOfCyclesToFailure.description: Total number of load cycles endured before failure (N_f). For runout specimens, this equals the predefined cycle limit.

failureCriterion.description: Criterion used to define failure (e.g., complete separation, stiffness drop, specified crack length).

sNCurve.description: Parameters of the S-N (Wöhler) curve derived from the test campaign.

basquinEquation.description: Basquin equation parameters for stress-controlled (HCF) tests: σ_a = σ'_f * (N_f)^b.

fatigueStrengthExponent.description: Fatigue strength exponent (b), dimensionless. Typically negative.

coffinMansonParameters.description: Coffin-Manson parameters for strain-controlled (LCF) tests: ε_p = ε'_f * (N_f)^c.

fatigueDuctilityExponent.description: Fatigue ductility exponent (c), dimensionless. Typically negative.

fatigueLimit.description: Fatigue limit or endurance limit in megapascals (MPa). Applicable to materials that exhibit a true fatigue limit (e.g., ferrous alloys).

failureMode.description: Details of the failure mode and fracture characteristics, typically documented via SEM fractography.

initiationSite.description: Location where the fatigue crack initiated.

fractureSurfaceCharacteristics.description: Description of the fracture surface (e.g., striations, beach marks, river patterns, dimples).
